title: RUN --mount=bind readwrite (rw) changes aren't reflected on host
body:
### Issue Description

After executing the following Dockerfile, `hi` should exist on the host:

```Dockerfile
FROM registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed:latest
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/test,rw touch /test/hi;
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

```sh
echo -e 'FROM registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed:latest\nRUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,target=/test,rw touch /test/hi;' > Dockerfile
podman build .
ls -l
```

### Describe the results you received

```
Dockerfile
```


### Describe the results you expected

```
Dockerfile
hi
```

### podman info output

```yaml
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.40.1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.1.10-150500.9.9.1.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.10, commit: unknown'
  distribution:
    distribution: opensuse-leap
    version: "15.6"
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.2.6-150000.73.2.x86_64
version:
  APIVersion: 5.5.2
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: it is the same issue as #5951

Oh, my. This is _really_ counter-intuitive. For others, finding this bug after a lot of bug hunting: this is the expected behaviour.

Bind mounting a directory doesn't write back anything to the mounted directory. In other words `--mount=type=bind != mount -o bind`.
